required_sections.notes: LOs: 5 verb-object LOs. Intro: ~200w, friend-analogy lead. Core Concepts: 6 subsections ~2200w. Key Takeaways: 5 bullets ~120w. All non-trivial.

sequence_drift.notes: All 14.1/14.2 terms used freely. scope_creep.notes: Scope tight: why hallucination happens + how RAG mechanism reduces it. §5.5 remaining risks on-headline. §6 conceptual only. No fine-tuning, RLHF, architecture taught. context window named-and-deferred.

beginner_readability.notes: All new terms defined on first use. Friend analogy leads introduction. Concrete refund-policy example in §6. No jargon dumps. Graceful degradation, auditability, factual grounding all defined inline.

verdict: PASS (cycle 1) — all 6 checks green. 3420w standard, all 5 primary cited (rate >= 88%), no forward refs, scope tight, strong beginner readability. Hallucination mechanism explained with friend analogy + open-book exam analogy. Context window introduced and deferred correctly.
